evals.prompt: We have one repo with Node-run ESM scripts, browser modules, and userscripts. Explain how to split the tsconfig setup and when .mts is justified.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that uses separate tsconfig projects for the different runtime surfaces, treats .mts as a deliberate Node ESM choice, and keeps runtime assumptions explicit.
assertions: The response recommends separate tsconfig projects or equivalent runtime separation
The response explains .mts as a specific Node ESM or explicit module-format choice
The response distinguishes browser modules or userscripts from Node-run scripts

evals.prompt: Review this strict TypeScript package layout and tell me whether the shared types and runtime validation boundaries are in the right place.
evals.expected_output: A review that keeps types close to the feature, preserves runtime validation at trust boundaries, and avoids over-centralized shared type buckets.
assertions: The response keeps runtime validation explicit for untrusted input
The response discusses whether types are too centralized or too detached from the owning feature
The response avoids recommending type-level cleverness as the default fix
